item.description:
# DisplayHelper Pro 1.1.1
## What's New
- Adds optional Auto Wi-Fi Switch for saved Wi-Fi networks, with a signal threshold and conservative scans. - Shows current and alternative Wi-Fi network details in the menu using public macOS Wi-Fi APIs. - Keeps Wi-Fi passwords out of scope; the app does not read, store, or send Wi-Fi passwords. - Avoids showing a Keychain permission prompt during launch if an older local build created the saved license item. - Hides the saved license key in the Manage License window. - Validates licenses from the live DisplayHelper Pro Lemon Squeezy product. - Adds a Buy License button to the Manage License window. - Enables Start at Login by default on first launch while preserving later user changes. - Fixes duplicate "You're up to date!" dialogs when checking for updates. - Uses `DisplayHelper Pro.app` as the installed app name. - Refines the compact menu with About, feedback, privacy, license, and update actions. - Ships in a modern drag-to-Applications DMG.
## Install
1. Open the DMG. 2. Drag `DisplayHelper Pro.app` onto `Applications`. 3. Open DisplayHelper Pro from Applications.
The app appears in the macOS menu bar.
## Compatibility
Compatibility note: This app depends on macOS display brightness behavior that may change in future macOS updates.
Requires macOS 13.0 or later.
